texto: I wanted to throw off the chains that bound me and give my entire being to someone. I wanted to know what it felt like to love another so much that the whole world came crashing down around me when it ended. I wanted obsession. I wanted madness. I wanted something all-consuming. And now that I realized it, I didn’t think that I would ever be the same again. And that terrified me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Navessa Allen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I wanted to throw off the chains that bound me and give my entire being to someone. I wanted to k…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, chains, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e love.

texto: Never attempt to apply logic to madness, for there is none; it is the nature of madness to be illogical.
explicacao: Citação de Navessa Allen sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Never attempt to apply logic to madness, for there is none; it is the nature of madness to be ill…” articula reflexao, insanity, madness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
